items.name: Scrapling
items.description: Adaptive Python web scraping with resilient selectors, stealth fetchers, spiders, CLI extraction, and MCP support.
items.long_description:
# Scrapling

Adaptive Python web scraping for resilient selectors, browser-aware fetching, full spider crawls, CLI extraction, and MCP-assisted data collection.

## What you can do

- **Adaptive selectors**: Save and relocate target elements when a site changes its DOM.
- **Fetchers**: Use fast HTTP requests, dynamic browser rendering, or stealth browser sessions as the target requires.
- **Spiders**: Build concurrent crawls with sessions, throttling, pause/resume, and export pipelines.
- **CLI extraction**: Pull URL content into markdown, text, or selector-scoped files without writing a script.
- **MCP support**: Let agents extract targeted page content before passing compact results into chat.

## Prerequisites

```bash
pip install "scrapling[all]"
scrapling install
```

For parser-only usage, install `scrapling` without extras. For browser fetchers, MCP, or the shell, use the relevant extras from the skill instructions.

## Example Requests

- "Scrape product titles and prices from this public catalog."
- "Build a resilient Scrapling spider for these documentation pages."
- "Use adaptive selectors so this scraper survives DOM changes."
- "Extract this page to markdown with the Scrapling CLI."

Use only on public or authorized targets, with respectful rate limits and site policy compliance.

items.name: Career-Ops
items.description: AI-powered job search operations: evaluate roles, tailor CVs, scan portals, prep interviews, and track applications.
items.long_description:
# Career-Ops

AI-powered job search operations for candidates who want a disciplined local pipeline rather than spray-and-pray applications.

## What you can do

- **Evaluate roles**: Score job descriptions across fit, gaps, level, compensation, and strategy.
- **Tailor CVs**: Generate ATS-friendly PDFs from a local markdown CV and role-specific evidence.
- **Scan portals**: Monitor configured company and job-board sources such as Greenhouse, Ashby, Lever, Wellfound, and company pages.
- **Batch process**: Evaluate multiple listings and keep results resumable.
- **Prepare interviews**: Build STAR+Reflection stories, company research, outreach drafts, and negotiation scripts.
- **Track applications**: Keep a local source of truth with deduplication, status normalization, and a Go terminal dashboard.

## Prerequisites

```powershell
$dest = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE "Developer\career-ops"
git clone https://github.com/santifer/career-ops.git $dest
cd $dest
npm install
npx playwright install chromium
npm run doctor
```

Then copy the example profile and portal templates, add `cv.md`, and customize the system with your target roles and constraints.

## Example Requests

- "Evaluate this job URL with Career-Ops and tell me whether it is worth applying."
- "Tailor my CV PDF for this role, but keep every claim truthful."
- "Scan my configured AI company portals and add promising roles to the tracker."
- "Create interview prep and negotiation notes for this opportunity."

Career-Ops keeps humans in the loop: review all generated content before applying, sending outreach, or changing real application status.
items.author: santifer / RAPR AI

install_commands.windows_cmd: $ErrorActionPreference="Stop"; $dest=Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE "Developer\career-ops"; if(!(Test-Path $dest)){ New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force (Split-Path $dest) | Out-Null; git clone https://github.com/santifer/career-ops.git $dest }; Push-Location $dest; npm install; Pop-Location

items.name: PDFCraft
items.description: Privacy-focused browser PDF toolkit with 90+ tools for editing, converting, merging, splitting, securing, and workflow automation.
items.long_description:
# PDFCraft

A privacy-focused browser PDF toolkit for users who need professional document operations without uploading files to external PDF services.

## What you can do

- **Organize PDFs**: Merge, split, extract, rotate, reorder, delete, compare, booklet, OCR, metadata, and attachments.
- **Edit and annotate**: Add text, images, signatures, watermarks, headers, footers, stamps, forms, bookmarks, and page numbers.
- **Convert files**: Convert images, Markdown, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, ebooks, emails, and other formats to PDF, or export PDF content to images, Office formats, JSON, PDF/A, tables, and more.
- **Optimize and repair**: Compress, repair, linearize, rasterize, standardize page dimensions, and outline fonts.
- **Secure documents**: Encrypt, decrypt, sanitize, flatten, remove metadata, and set permissions.
- **Automate workflows**: Use the beta visual workflow editor for reusable document pipelines and batch processing.

## Prerequisites

Run locally:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/PDFCraftTool/pdfcraft.git
cd pdfcraft && npm install
npm run dev
```

Or run the Docker image:

```bash
docker pull ghcr.io/pdfcrafttool/pdfcraft:latest
docker run -d -p 8080:80 --name pdfcraft ghcr.io/pdfcrafttool/pdfcraft:latest
```

## Example Requests

- "Run PDFCraft locally so I can process sensitive PDFs on-device."
- "Set up the PDFCraft Docker container on port 8080."
- "Use PDFCraft-style tools to design a workflow for merging, compressing, watermarking, and securing documents."
- "Help customize the PDFCraft Next.js app or add a new PDF tool."

For sensitive files, keep processing local, preserve originals, and review outputs before sharing.

# gstack

Garry Tan's opinionated Claude Code setup for turning one AI coding session into a structured software-factory workflow.

## What you can do

- **Plan like a product team**: Office hours, CEO review, engineering review, design review, DevEx review, and design consultation.
- **Review like a senior team**: Code review, security review, careful investigation, guard/freeze/unfreeze flows, and production-risk checks.
- **QA in a real browser**: Browser setup, QA-only testing, cookie setup, browsing workflows, and canary checks.
- **Ship with structure**: Release docs, landing reports, deploy setup, ship commands, and land-and-deploy workflows.
- **Share with a team**: Team-mode bootstrap for repos that should require or recommend gstack in Claude Code.

## Prerequisites

Requirements: Claude Code, Git, Bun v1.0+, and Node.js on Windows.

```bash
git clone --single-branch --depth 1 https://github.com/garrytan/gstack.git ~/.claude/skills/gstack
cd ~/.claude/skills/gstack
./setup
```

Team mode from inside a repo:

```bash
(cd ~/.claude/skills/gstack && ./setup --team) && ~/.claude/skills/gstack/bin/gstack-team-init required
```

## Example Requests

- "Install gstack for Claude Code on this machine."
- "Set up team mode so this repo recommends gstack."
- "Use the gstack review workflow to check this branch."
- "Adapt gstack-style CEO, design, QA, and release reviews for this project."

Use gstack for Claude Code workflows specifically; for Codex, translate the same review and QA intent into Codex-native steps unless the user asks to edit Claude-specific files.

items.name: Browser Harness
items.description: Self-healing CDP harness that lets agents drive a real Chrome browser and write missing browser helpers during execution.
items.long_description:
# Browser Harness

Browser Harness connects an AI agent directly to Chrome or a Browser Use cloud browser through a thin CDP harness. It is useful when an agent needs real browser state, authenticated sessions, file uploads, debugging, scraping, QA, or site-specific workflows that can improve over repeated runs.

## What you can do

- **Use a real browser**: Connect to an existing Chrome profile with cookies and logged-in state.
- **Automate messy workflows**: Let the agent inspect pages, click, type, upload files, handle tabs, and recover from missing helpers.
- **Build reusable helpers**: Keep task-specific logic in `agent-workspace/agent_helpers.py` and optional domain skills.
- **Run local or cloud browsers**: Use local Chrome for watched tasks, or Browser Use cloud when you need isolated/headless sessions.

## Windows Setup

The RAPR skill installs immediately. The installer can clone Browser Harness and install the `browser-harness` command when Git and uv are available. Connecting to your real Chrome browser still requires user consent.

```powershell
$dest = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE "Developer\browser-harness"
git clone https://github.com/browser-use/browser-harness $dest
cd $dest
uv tool install -e .
browser-harness --doctor
```

Then open `chrome://inspect/#remote-debugging` in Chrome and allow remote debugging for the profile you want the agent to use.

## Example Requests

- "Use Browser Harness to log into my dashboard and export the latest report."
- "Run a browser QA pass against localhost and fix any obvious UI breakage."
- "Automate this multi-step website workflow using my existing Chrome session."

Only connect to accounts and sites the user authorizes. Prefer a separate browser profile for sensitive tasks, and do not silently operate on logged-in accounts.
items.author: browser-use / RAPR AI

install_commands.label: Verify Git and uv are available
Clone and install Browser Harness command
install_commands.windows_cmd: $ErrorActionPreference="Stop"; $dest=Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE "Developer\browser-harness"; if(!(Test-Path $dest)){ New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force (Split-Path $dest) | Out-Null; git clone https://github.com/browser-use/browser-harness $dest }; Push-Location $dest; uv tool install -e .; Pop-Location

install_commands.label: Allow Chrome remote debugging when ready
install_commands.instructions: Open chrome://inspect/#remote-debugging in Chrome, tick the remote debugging permission, and click Allow when Chrome prompts. Run browser-harness --doctor afterwards.

auth_info.description: No account is required for local Chrome. Browser Use cloud browsers require BROWSER_USE_API_KEY.

items.name: pywinauto / UIAutomation
items.description: Windows desktop UI automation with pywinauto, Microsoft UI Automation, and Python uiautomation.
items.long_description:
# pywinauto / UIAutomation

Windows desktop automation for agents and scripts that need to inspect or control native applications through Win32 and Microsoft UI Automation. This combines pywinauto for high-level app/window control with Python UIAutomation for lower-level UIA tree access.

## What you can do

- **Inspect Windows apps**: Enumerate windows, controls, names, AutomationIds, and class names.
- **Automate native workflows**: Click, type, select, focus, and read state from desktop applications.
- **Test desktop UI**: Build regression checks for dialogs, installers, admin tools, and internal apps.
- **Bridge agent control**: Give RAPR agents a Windows UIA-first route before falling back to screenshots/OCR.

## Windows Setup

The installer can install the Python packages when Python is available:

```powershell
python -m pip install pywinauto uiautomation pywin32 comtypes
```

A real unlocked interactive desktop session is required. UIA automation will not behave reliably from Session 0, Windows services, or a locked desktop.

## Example Requests

- "Inspect the visible controls in this Windows app and tell me what can be automated."
- "Build a pywinauto script for this repeatable desktop workflow."
- "Use UIAutomation to read the current app state before taking action."

Ask before interacting with sensitive apps, destructive dialogs, admin prompts, payments, emails, or production systems.
items.author: pywinauto + Python UIAutomation / RAPR AI

install_commands.label: Install pywinauto and UIAutomation packages
install_commands.windows_cmd: python -m pip install pywinauto uiautomation pywin32 comtypes
install_commands.windows_check_cmd: python -c "import pywinauto, uiautomation; print('windows ui automation ok')"

install_commands.label: Use an unlocked interactive desktop session
install_commands.windows_cmd: python -c "print('Run UI automation only from an unlocked interactive Windows desktop session.')"
install_commands.instructions: Run UI automation from an unlocked interactive Windows desktop session. Avoid Windows services, Session 0, locked desktops, and elevation mismatches.

auth_info.description: No account required. Target apps may require their own login or admin elevation.

items.name: OmniParser
items.description: Microsoft screen parsing for vision-based GUI agents and computer-use workflows.
items.long_description:
# OmniParser

OmniParser is Microsoft's screen parsing tool for pure vision-based GUI agents. It parses UI screenshots into structured, easy-to-understand elements so agents can reason about screen regions before acting.

## What you can do

- **Parse screenshots**: Detect UI elements, text, icons, and interactable regions.
- **Ground computer-use agents**: Convert visual screens into structured regions for downstream actions.
- **Add visual fallback**: Help when DOM selectors or Windows UI Automation trees are unavailable.
- **Prototype agent perception**: Pair with Browser Harness, pywinauto/UIAutomation, Playwright, or another controller.

## Windows Setup

The RAPR skill installs immediately. Full OmniParser setup is manual because it requires a repo clone, Python/conda environment, model weights, and possibly GPU/CUDA choices.

```powershell
$dest = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE "Developer\OmniParser"
git clone https://github.com/microsoft/OmniParser.git $dest
cd $dest
conda create -n omni python==3.12
conda activate omni
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

Then download V2 weights into `weights` using the upstream README instructions.

## Example Requests

- "Parse this screenshot and identify the clickable UI regions."
- "Use OmniParser as the perception layer for a desktop automation workflow."
- "Compare OmniParser output with the Windows UIA tree and choose the safer action target."

Keep screenshots local unless the user approves upload. Review model checkpoint licenses before redistribution or commercial deployment.
items.author: Microsoft / RAPR AI
items.pricing_note: Free & Open Source; model checkpoints have their own licenses

install_commands.instructions: From %USERPROFILE%\Developer\OmniParser, create the conda environment, install requirements, then download V2 weights into the weights folder using the upstream README commands. This may be large and license-sensitive.

# Screenpipe

Local-first desktop memory for AI agents. Screenpipe records screen, audio, app switches, OCR, accessibility tree data, and transcriptions locally so agents can search what happened on the workstation and trigger automations from real activity.

## What you can do

- **Remember desktop activity**: Capture what the user saw, heard, typed, and switched between.
- **Search with AI**: Ask natural-language questions over recent or historical activity.
- **Power agents with context**: Expose the local activity stream through MCP.
- **Build pipes**: Run automations triggered by work activity.
- **Keep data local**: Use local storage, offline support, filters, and optional encryption.

## Windows Setup

The RAPR skill installs immediately. Screenpipe recording is manual because it is a long-running local service.

```powershell
npx -y screenpipe@latest record
claude mcp add screenpipe -- npx -y screenpipe-mcp
```

## Example Requests

- "Summarize what I worked on in the last hour."
- "Find the meeting where the pricing decision was discussed."
- "Create an automation that updates my task tracker when I work on a specific project."

Ask users to configure recording scope, filters, retention, encryption, and consent before capturing sensitive activity. Do not enable always-on recording silently.

# InsForge

Agent-native backend platform for shipping full-stack apps with AI coding agents. InsForge gives agents structured access to database, auth, storage, compute, hosting, logs, services, and MCP-aware backend context.

## What you can do

- **Provision a backend**: Run Postgres-based backend infrastructure locally or through cloud-hosted InsForge.
- **Connect AI agents**: Use the InsForge MCP server so coding agents can inspect schemas, permissions, logs, docs, and services.
- **Build full-stack apps**: Pair RAPR sessions with a backend designed for agentic development.

git clone https://github.com/InsForge/InsForge.git

docker compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up

Open `http://localhost:7130`, then connect the InsForge MCP server from the UI.

- **Design adversarial QA** for browser agents.

- **Create guardrails** around consent and irreversible actions.

items.name: MOSS-TTS
items.description: Local open-source text-to-speech with MOSS-TTS-Nano, voice cloning, multilingual speech, dialogue, and sound effects workflows.
items.long_description:
# MOSS-TTS

Generate local multilingual speech with the open-source MOSS-TTS family from MOSI.AI and OpenMOSS. This skill focuses on MOSS-TTS-Nano first because it is lightweight, CPU friendly, and practical for local RAPR workflows, while documenting upgrade paths for the full MOSS-TTS family.

## What you can do

- **Local TTS**: Convert text into speech without a hosted API.
- **CPU-friendly demos**: Use MOSS-TTS-Nano ONNX for lightweight local inference.
- **Voice cloning**: Generate speech from a short local reference audio clip when you have rights or consent.
- **Multilingual narration**: Work across English, Chinese, and other supported languages.
- **Dialogue workflows**: Use MOSS-TTSD for expressive multi-speaker spoken dialogue when you need the heavier model path.
- **Audio generation**: Explore the broader MOSS-TTS family for voice design and sound effect generation.

## Prerequisites

A clean Python environment is recommended. The upstream project recommends Conda for the easiest setup:

```bash
conda create -n moss-tts-nano python=3.12 -y
conda activate moss-tts-nano
git clone https://github.com/OpenMOSS/MOSS-TTS-Nano.git
cd MOSS-TTS-Nano
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install -e .
```

For local CPU inference, start with the ONNX entrypoints:

```bash
python infer_onnx.py --prompt-audio-path assets/audio/zh_1.wav --text "Welcome to MOSS-TTS-Nano."
python app_onnx.py
```

Then open `http://127.0.0.1:18083` for the local web demo.

## Example Requests

- "Generate a narration WAV from this paragraph using MOSS-TTS-Nano."
- "Start the local MOSS-TTS web demo."
- "Use this reference audio to clone a voice for a consented demo."
- "Create separate audio files for each section of this article."
- "Compare the Nano CPU workflow with the heavier MOSS-TTS dialogue model."

Use voice cloning only with consent and appropriate rights. Do not use this skill for impersonation, fraud, or bypassing identity checks.

items.name: Playwright CLI
items.description: AI-driven browser automation — navigate, click, fill forms, scrape content, take screenshots via Playwright CLI.
items.long_description:
# Playwright CLI

Give any AI session the power to automate a real browser. Navigate pages, fill forms, click buttons, scrape data, and take screenshots — all via fast, token-efficient CLI commands.

## Why Playwright CLI?

Unlike browser tools that stream entire page accessibility trees into your AI's context, Playwright CLI saves state to disk and uses short shell commands. This makes it **~4x more token-efficient** while keeping full browser automation power.

## What you can do

- **Navigate**: Open URLs, go back/forward, manage tabs
- **Interact**: Click, fill, type, select, check, drag & drop
- **Inspect**: Snapshot pages to get element references, take screenshots
- **Scrape**: Extract structured content from any website
- **Automate**: Login flows, form submissions, multi-page workflows

## How it works

1. Install the skill from the RAPR AI marketplace
2. Make sure Node.js is installed (`node --version`)
3. Install Playwright CLI: `npm install -g @playwright/cli@latest`
4. Install browser: `npx playwright install chromium`
5. Ask any AI to do a browser task — the skill auto-activates!

## Example Commands

- "Go to google.com and search for RAPR AI reviews"
- "Open example.com and fill out the contact form"
- "Scrape the pricing table from that website"
- "Take a screenshot of the landing page"
- "Automate login and download my invoice"
items.author: RAPR AI

auth_info.description: No login needed. Playwright runs locally with the bundled Chromium browser.

# Ruflo

Coordinate advanced multi-agent workflows with Ruflo, an open-source orchestration platform for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and MCP-compatible environments. Ruflo is best for users who need delegated specialists, shared memory, swarms, and autonomous workflow coordination.

## What you can do

- **Spawn swarms**: Coordinate planner, coder, reviewer, tester, security, and DevOps agents.
- **Delegate work**: Break complex tasks into specialist roles with explicit boundaries.
- **Use MCP workflows**: Connect Ruflo to MCP-compatible clients and tools.
- **Share memory**: Experiment with persistent context across agent runs.
- **Automate pipelines**: Build multi-step workflows with routing, retries, review, and stop conditions.

## Prerequisites

```bash
npm install -g ruflo
ruflo init
```

Verify the CLI before running swarms:

```bash
ruflo --help
ruflo status
```

## Example Requests

- "Initialize Ruflo for this repo and show me the MCP setup."
- "Spawn a small swarm to review the auth module."
- "Design a Ruflo workflow for planning, coding, review, and tests."
- "Audit whether Ruflo is adding too much token overhead."
- "Set safe boundaries for a multi-agent coding workflow."

Ruflo can increase context and token usage. Start with small bounded swarms, confirm persistent memory before enabling it, and review generated commands before execution.

Give local AI models the ability to search the web. This skill is specifically for AIs that don't have built-in search — like Ollama, NemoClaw, and other local/sandboxed models.

**Note:** Cloud AIs (Claude, Gemini, Codex) already have native web search built in. They don't need this skill.

## What you can do

- **Search**: Query DuckDuckGo, Brave, or Google from the command line
- **News**: Get current events and recent information
- **Weather**: Check weather for any location
- **Docs**: Look up documentation and technical references
- **Facts**: Quick answers to factual questions

## Prerequisites

No API key needed — uses DuckDuckGo by default (free, no sign-up).

Optionally, get a free Brave Search API key at [brave.com/search/api](https://brave.com/search/api/) for better results.

## Example Commands

- "Search for the latest AI news"
- "What's the weather in Delhi?"
- "Look up Next.js app router documentation"
- "Find the current population of India"

- **OBS Studio** — Screen recording & streaming

# OpenCLI

A universal CLI hub that turns any website, Electron app, or local binary into a standardized command-line interface.

OpenCLI provides a single unified CLI that can interact with websites (Reddit, Hacker News, Twitter/X, YouTube, etc.), wrap Electron apps, and passthrough to existing CLI tools — all with a consistent interface. If a required tool isn't installed, OpenCLI can auto-install it.

- **Unified discovery** — `opencli list` shows all available commands
- **Auto-install** — Missing tools are installed automatically
- **Multiple output formats** — JSON, YAML, Markdown, CSV, table

- **AI-native** — Built with AGENT.md for seamless AI agent integration

2. Make sure Node.js 16+ is installed
3. OpenCLI is installed automatically: `npm install -g @jackwener/opencli`
4. Ask any AI to fetch data from websites or use CLI tools — the skill auto-activates!

- "Search YouTube for AI agent tutorials"
- "Show trending posts on Reddit r/technology"

items.description: Fast Rust CLI for AI-controlled Chrome automation via ref-based snapshots, semantic locators, network interception, and cloud browser providers.
items.long_description:
# Agent Browser

A fast Rust CLI that gives AI agents deterministic, stable control over Chrome/Chromium via the Chrome DevTools Protocol.

## What you can do

- **Ref-based snapshots**: Get stable element refs from the accessibility tree and act on them without fragile CSS selectors.
- **Semantic locators**: Find elements by role, text, label, placeholder, alt text, or test ID.
- **Network interception**: Mock responses, block requests, and record HAR files.
- **Auth persistence**: Chrome profiles, session state, and encrypted cookie management.
- **React introspection**: Component tree inspection, render profiling, Suspense boundaries.
- **Web Vitals**: LCP, CLS, TTFB, INP performance monitoring.
- **Cloud providers**: Connect to Browserless, Browserbase, Browser Use, Kernel, and AgentCore.
- **Natural language control**: Use `agent-browser chat` for AI-driven browsing.
- **Streaming viewport**: Live WebSocket preview for pair-browsing sessions.

## Prerequisites

```bash
npm install -g agent-browser
agent-browser install
```

Linux users: `agent-browser install --with-deps`

## Example Requests

- "Use agent-browser to snapshot this page and click the login button."
- "Intercept and mock the API response at /api/users."
- "Run a Web Vitals check on my site and report LCP and CLS."
- "Set up a persistent Chrome profile for this session so I don't have to re-login."

Always take a snapshot before acting on elements; refs are stable only within a single snapshot cycle.

items.long_description:
# SigMap

Ranks codebase files against a natural language query using TF-IDF, extracts function and class signatures from the top matches, and generates compact context files for AI assistants.

## What you can do

- **Query-driven file ranking**: Ask "Where is auth handled?" and get the top 5 most relevant files with relevance scores.
- **Token reduction**: Compress 80K+ token repos down to 2K-4K tokens with 40-98% reduction.
- **Adapter outputs**: Generate CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md, and more.
- **Validation**: Confirm the right files are in scope before a big refactor or debug session.
- **Groundedness scoring**: Judge whether an AI response is grounded in actual codebase context.
- **MCP server**: 9 on-demand tools for Claude Code and Cursor integration.
- **Zero dependencies**: Works offline with no external services.

## Prerequisites

```bash
npm install -g sigmap
sigmap --health
```

Or use without installing: `npx sigmap`

## Example Requests

- "Run sigmap to generate a CLAUDE.md for this repo."
- "Ask sigmap which files handle payment processing."
- "Validate that the right files are in scope for this auth refactor."
- "Set up sigmap as an MCP server for Claude Code."

Regenerate context files after major structural changes to keep AI answers accurate.

items.description: Create retention-optimized TikTok, Reels, and YouTube Shorts with hooks, captions, cuts, audio cues, and Remotion rendering.
items.long_description:
# Viral Shorts

Turn raw clips into retention-optimized vertical shorts for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts using a practical short-form video workflow plus a bundled Remotion template.

## What you can do

- **Find the hook**: Identify the strongest 1-2 second opener and move it to the front.
- **Build retention edits**: Plan cuts, punch-in zooms, camera moves, caption beats, sound effects, and loop mechanics.
- **Burn in captions**: Use word-level Whisper captions with TikTok-style active-word highlighting.
- **Render with Remotion**: Start from the included Remotion project and drive the edit from a typed `editConfig.ts` file.
- **Adapt by niche**: Apply different guidance for talking-head clips, podcast cutdowns, faceless videos, family content, and general social posts.
- **Handle rights honestly**: Use royalty-free or licensed audio and understand the risks around movie, TV, music, and third-party footage.

## Prerequisites

The skill includes a ready Remotion project under `assets/remotion-viral-short/`. For rendering, install its dependencies when you use the template:

```bash
cd assets/remotion-viral-short
npm install
npm run dev
```

For transcription, the template uses Remotion's Whisper.cpp tooling and creates word-level caption JSON from the raw clip.

## Example Requests

- "Edit this talking-head clip into a TikTok with captions and a strong hook."
- "Cut this podcast moment into a 30-second YouTube Short."
- "Make this raw video more retention-optimized for Instagram Reels."
- "Add word-by-word captions, punch-ins, sound effects, and a seamless loop."
- "Review why my short flopped and suggest a better hook."

# OpenViking

OpenViking is an open-source hierarchical context database and memory management layer for AI agents. It uses a structured `viking://` URI filesystem layout to organize agent knowledge, assets, and guidelines. By utilizing tiered (L0/L1/L2) memory retrieval, it reduces token usage and improves retrieval accuracy over standard flat vector databases.

## What you can do

- **Hierarchical Memory**: Organize memories and tasks in a filesystem-like layout.
- **Context Tiering**: Retrieve only the context tier you need (L0 fast memory, L1 project docs, L2 heavy archives).
- **Token Saving**: Load precise context subsets instead of dumping entire directories into LLM prompts.

## Installation

Install using the marketplace install button or run `rapr install openviking`.

items.name: Zapier MCP
items.description: Built-in universal connector for thousands of app actions through your Zapier MCP server.
items.long_description:
# Zapier MCP

Zapier MCP is RAPR AI's universal connector for the long tail of SaaS apps. It is not an installable `.raprpkg`; it ships as core runtime infrastructure.

## What you can do

- **Connect one server URL**: Create a Zapier MCP server at `mcp.zapier.com`, enable the app actions you want, then paste the server URL into RAPR AI.
- **Reach thousands of apps**: Zapier MCP can expose enabled tools across 9,000+ apps and 30,000+ actions.
- **Use every AI runner**: Once connected, Zapier tools appear as `zapier_*` MCP tools for Claude, Gemini, Codex, Ollama, and other supported runners.
- **Keep secrets out of config**: RAPR AI stores the per-user Zapier MCP URL in the encrypted token vault and keeps `mcp_servers.json` on a `${ZAPIER_MCP_URL}` placeholder.

## How it works

RAPR AI bridges the hosted Zapier MCP endpoint through `npx -y mcp-remote ${ZAPIER_MCP_URL}`. This replaces the old Zapier NLA plugin, which remains disabled because Zapier retired NLA.

## Setup

1. Open RAPR AI and go to Packages.
2. Find the Zapier card in installed MCP connectors.
3. Click Connect.
4. Paste your full Zapier MCP Server URL from `https://mcp.zapier.com`.

Zapier task usage and connected app permissions are governed by your Zapier account and the tools you enable on that server.
items.author: RAPR AI

install_commands.instructions: Open Packages, choose Zapier, click Connect, and paste your Zapier MCP Server URL from https://mcp.zapier.com.

items.name: MoneyPrinterTurbo
items.description: Generate AI short videos from topics or scripts, then polish the best drafts with Viral Shorts.
items.long_description:
# MoneyPrinterTurbo

Generate complete short videos from a topic, keyword, or script using the open-source MoneyPrinterTurbo engine, then optionally run a Viral Shorts polish pass for retention-focused social output.

## What you can do

- **Topic to video**: Generate scripts, TTS voiceover, subtitles, background music, stock footage, and a finished short from one idea.
- **Batch variants**: Create multiple drafts so you can choose the strongest script and visual match.
- **Faceless content**: Produce stock-footage shorts for explainers, motivation, education, product ideas, and social channels.
- **WebUI or API**: Run MoneyPrinterTurbo through its Streamlit interface or FastAPI endpoints for repeatable pipelines.
- **Polish with Viral Shorts**: Use the best MoneyPrinterTurbo draft as raw material for hooks, word-by-word captions, punch-ins, SFX, and loop design.

## Best paired with Viral Shorts

MoneyPrinterTurbo is the factory. Viral Shorts is the editor. Use MoneyPrinterTurbo when you need speed and automation; add Viral Shorts when you need platform-native pacing, stronger first seconds, retention beats, and a less generic final cut.

## Prerequisites

MoneyPrinterTurbo is an external open-source project. Install it locally when you want to render videos:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/harry0703/MoneyPrinterTurbo.git
cd MoneyPrinterTurbo
uv python install 3.11
uv sync --frozen
```

Then configure `config.toml` with your LLM provider, API keys, and optional Pexels keys.

## Example Requests

- "Generate three faceless Shorts drafts about AI productivity."
- "Use MoneyPrinterTurbo for the first draft, then Viral Shorts for the final edit."
- "Start the MoneyPrinterTurbo WebUI so I can generate a video from a keyword."
- "Create a short from this script with TTS, subtitles, BGM, and stock footage."
- "Review this MoneyPrinterTurbo output and make it more retention-optimized."

## Notes

Verify music and footage rights before commercial use. MoneyPrinterTurbo automates production, while Viral Shorts improves the edit quality and retention odds; neither guarantees virality.
items.author: Harry0703 / RAPR AI

install_commands.label: Install MoneyPrinterTurbo dependencies
install_commands.windows_cmd: cd "$env:USERPROFILE\tools\MoneyPrinterTurbo"; uv python install 3.11; uv sync --frozen
install_commands.windows_check_cmd: cd "$env:USERPROFILE\tools\MoneyPrinterTurbo"; uv run python -c "import moviepy, fastapi, streamlit; print('ok')"

auth_info.description: No login is required for the RAPR skill itself. MoneyPrinterTurbo may need provider keys in config.toml for LLMs, stock footage, TTS, or subtitle modes.
